commands.review_summary:
This PR threads plugin ownership through plugin-owned channel schema validation diagnostics and adds regression tests for external Feishu/Lark schema and metadata-only shadow cases.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +76. Total +110 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is clear: current main drops the plugin owner when caching channel schemas and formats the resulting schema errors with the raw channel message formatter. I did not run the PR tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair lane is indicated; the remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof, not a narrow code defect.

target.title: Clarify plugin channel config additional-property errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Clarify plugin-owned channel config validation errors by naming the plugin that owns the selected channel schema.
- Keep validation behavior unchanged: unsupported properties still fail, and bundled/raw channel diagnostics remain plugin-agnostic.
- Cover the metadata-only shadow case so diagnostics stay assigned to the plugin that actually supplied the schema.

## Linked context

Related to #77116. This is a diagnostic improvement for the plugin-owned channel validation path. It does not change Feishu bundle data and does not relax external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` validation rules.

- Fix classification: root-cause diagnostic fix for plugin-owned channel schema validation.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high; the changed path is covered by regression tests for both the direct external schema case and the closer metadata-only shadow case.
- Root cause: plugin-aware channel validation selected the external plugin-owned channel schema, but diagnostics did not retain the schema owner consistently with the channel schema metadata selection contract.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the fix derives diagnostic ownership only from channel config entries that actually provide a schema, so the error source follows the same plugin-owned schema selected for validation instead of a nearer metadata-only channel entry.
- What did NOT change: validation semantics, Feishu bundle data, external plugin validation rules, Feishu runtime behavior, and raw bundled channel diagnostics are unchanged; this PR only changes diagnostic attribution for plugin-owned channel validation errors.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: when an external plugin-owned channel schema rejects an unsupported channel property, the diagnostic now names the plugin that supplied the schema. A closer metadata-only channel entry no longer gets blamed for schema errors it did not define.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node.js 22.22.0 with pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts src/config/validation*.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (copied terminal output):

```text
Exit code: 0
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  12 passed (12)
```

- Observed result after fix: the external Feishu/Lark schema test reports `invalid config for plugin openclaw-lark: must not have additional properties: "unsupportedField"`, including the rejected field name and the actual schema owner.
- What was not tested: no live Feishu workspace or external `@larksuite/openclaw-lark` network integration was exercised; this PR only covers the local plugin-aware channel schema validation diagnostic path.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/config/validation.channel-metadata.test.ts src/config/validation*.test.ts` -> `Exit code: 0`, `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 12 passed (12)`.
- `corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . run tsgo:core:test` -> `Exit code: 0`.
- `corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . run lint:core` -> `Exit code: 0`.
- Local Codex diff review against `origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues.

## Risk checklist

- Validation semantics are unchanged; this only changes the diagnostic prefix for plugin-owned channel validation errors.
- Bundled channel diagnostics stay plugin-agnostic.
- Feishu bundle data and external plugin validation rules are not changed.
- No token, credential, or network behavior is changed.

## Current review state

- Ready for maintainer review after local validation and local Codex diff review.
